description: The probability of the trinary sentiment score (above) matching a known sentiment state alone: 0.000 = zero probability, 1.000 is absolute probability.
Identifies if the item is headline only or if there is a body as well. "T" = true, for headline only, "F" = false, indicating there is a body to the story.
The estimated risk-adjusted 1 minute forward return for a given article as measured by stock return minus the stock beta multiplied market return, -5 being most negative impact and +5 most positive.
Identifies new stories: 1 = new story, 2 or greater means the item is linked to a previous story that was published within the last 24 hours.
Sentiment probability: The raw probability that the article sentiment is negative expressed as a decimal with all three probabilities summing to 1.
Sentiment probability: The raw probability that the article sentiment is neutral expressed as a decimal with all three probabilities summing to 1.
Sentiment probability: The raw probability that the article sentiment is positive expressed as a decimal with all three probabilities summing to 1.
Weighting of the sentiment to the company in that line of data. Relevance of "1.00" = primary relevance, any less indicates the appropriate degree of relevance.
Reveals in a trinary score the highest probable state of sentiment found in the news item, 1 = positive, 0 = neutral, and -1 = negative.
The publication timestamp of the news item in UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) by default.
Indicates whether the news story was automatically generated as part of a scheduled delivery (e.g., calendar event or systematic feed); 1 or "T" is scheduled, 0 or "F" is unscheduled
Indicates whether the news story was automatically generated as part of a scheduled delivery (e.g., calendar event or systematic feed); 1 or "T" is scheduled, 0 or "F" is unscheduled
Confidence level (0 to 1) that the sentiment score accurately matches the news item’s actual sentiment
Confidence level (0 to 1) that the sentiment score accurately matches the news item’s actual sentiment
Flag indicating if the item contains only a headline (1/“T”) or includes full article/text (0/“F”)
Flag indicating if the item contains only a headline (1/“T”) or includes full article/text (0/“F”)
Estimated risk-adjusted 1-minute forward return expected from the news event, roughly ranging from -5 to +5
Estimated risk-adjusted 1-minute forward return expected from the news event, roughly ranging from -5 to +5
Indicator of story novelty: 1 = first/new story, >1 = repeat or update linked to previous stories in last 24 hours
Indicator of story novelty: 1 = first/new story, >1 = repeat or update linked to previous stories in last 24 hours
Model-assigned probability (0 to 1) that the sentiment of the news article is negative; all three probabilities (positive, neutral, negative) sum to 1
Model-assigned probability (0 to 1) that the sentiment of the news article is negative; all three probabilities (positive, neutral, negative) sum to 1
The raw probability that the news article’s sentiment is classified as neutral, expressed as a decimal (with the probabilities for positive, neutral, and negative sentiment summing to 1)
The raw probability that the news article’s sentiment is classified as neutral, expressed as a decimal (with the probabilities for positive, neutral, and negative sentiment summing to 1)
Model-assigned probability (0 to 1) that the sentiment of the news article is positive; all three probabilities (positive, neutral, negative) sum to 1
Model-assigned probability (0 to 1) that the sentiment of the news article is positive; all three probabilities (positive, neutral, negative) sum to 1
Degree to which the news item focuses on the target company; 1 = primary, <1 = secondary/side mention
Degree to which the news item focuses on the target company; 1 = primary, <1 = secondary/side mention
Trinary sentiment classification for the news item: 1 = positive, 0 = neutral, -1 = negative
Trinary sentiment classification for the news item: 1 = positive, 0 = neutral, -1 = negative
UTC Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch) marking the exact publication time of the news article for point-in-time analysis
